body.title: Audit pass: build design patterns inventory
body.result: 11 patterns documented in docs/design_patterns_inventory.md with file refs and quick-reference table
body.intention: I intend that an audit pass scans the codebase and maintains a structured design patterns inventory that the pre-inference assembler can load without LLM involvement.
body.description:
Scan the UU/Igor codebase and produce a structured design patterns inventory doc. Identifies recurring patterns: BaseDevice/BaseShim lifecycle, IDLE loop, request/response envelope, registry pattern, flat-file boot state, device/shim boundary. Output doc is loaded by the pre-inference assembler at sprint start — no LLM needed to recognize patterns if the inventory is pre-built.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint; likely a new script under tools/ or lab/claudecode/, output to docs/design_patterns_inventory.md

**Design rules:** none apply — new file, no core device touches

**Scope boundary:** In scope: scan Python files in devices/ and unseen_university/, identify structural patterns, emit structured doc. Out of scope: pattern matching during sprint (that's the assembler), maintaining the inventory automatically (first version is a manual-run audit pass).

**Completion criteria:** Running the script produces docs/design_patterns_inventory.md listing ≥5 named patterns with example file references; the file is readable by the pre-inference assembler (T-pre-inference-assembler) as a plain-text context block.
